qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow denial of service in fromJS via deeply nested objects
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded recursive descent in fromJS() on deeply nested objects causing RangeError/denial of service. The advisory concerns prototype pollution through __proto__ assignment in mergeDeep(), mergeDeepWith(), merge(), Map.toJS(), and Map.toObject(); the vulnerable API, control flow, root cause, and security consequence are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described prototype-pollution root cause in immutable's merge* or Map.toJS()/toObject() APIs. The only final qualifying finding identifies a separate fromJS() stack-overflow denial-of-service condition.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-wf6x-7x77-mvgw describes prototype pollution via __proto__ handling in mergeDeep(), mergeDeepWith(), merge(), Map.toJS(), and Map.toObject().
Final finding fromJS-stack-overflow-dos reports unbounded recursive descent in src/fromJS.js leading to RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded for deeply nested input.
The final finding does not describe the advisory's __proto__ assignment path, missing guard, affected merge APIs, or Map.toJS()/toObject() prototype-pollution sink.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matched the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
